tool_name: Process Capability (Cp / Cpk / Pp / Ppk), PPM Defect Rate & Quality Loss Function

inputs.name: Upper Specification Limit (USL)
inputs.hint: From engineering drawing or customer specification
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification limit, zero uncertainty

inputs.name: Lower Specification Limit (LSL)
inputs.hint: From engineering drawing or customer specification
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification limit, zero uncertainty

inputs.name: Process Mean (X̄)
inputs.hint: Grand average of sample measurements — from SPC chart or data set
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Within-Subgroup (Short-Term) Std Dev (σ̂)
inputs.hint: σ̂ = R̄/d2 from X̄-R chart — represents inherent process variation only
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Overall (Long-Term) Std Dev (s)
inputs.hint: Sample std dev of all data — for Pp/Ppk calculation. If blank, Cpk = Ppk assumed.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Subgroup Sample Size (n)
inputs.hint: AIAG SPC: recommended n=5 for X̄-R | n=10 for X̄-S
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Total Number of Samples Measured (N)
inputs.hint: AIAG SPC: minimum 25 subgroups × n samples for valid Cpk
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Cost per Defective Part (scrap + rework + warranty)
inputs.hint: Include: scrap material + rework labour + warranty + customer return penalty
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Target Cpk Requirement
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Target Cpk Numerical Value
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

formulas: Tolerance_Band = usl - lsl   // [unit] Total tolerance width
Cp = Tolerance_Band / (6 * within_sigma)   // [—] Process capability ratio (potential)
Z_USL = (usl - process_mean) / within_sigma   // [—] Distance to USL in sigma units
Z_LSL = (process_mean - lsl) / within_sigma   // [—] Distance to LSL in sigma units
Cpk = MIN(Z_USL, Z_LSL) / 3   // [—] Process capability index (actual) — AIAG SPC
Pp = overall_sigma > 0 ? Tolerance_Band / (6 * overall_sigma) : Cp   // [—] Process performance ratio (long-term)
Ppk = overall_sigma > 0 ? MIN((usl - process_mean) / (3 * overall_sigma), (process_mean - lsl) / (3 * overall_sigma)) : Cpk   // [—] Process performance index
Total_PPM = (P_defect_USL + P_defect_LSL) * 1000000   // [PPM] Defects per million opportunities
Sigma_Level_ST = Cpk * 3   // [σ] Short-term sigma level (without 1.5σ shift)
Sigma_Level_LT = Cpk * 3 - 1.5   // [σ] Long-term sigma level (with 1.5σ Motorola shift)
Daily_Quality_Cost_USD = Daily_Defects * cost_per_defective_usd   // [USD/day] Daily quality loss
Annual_Quality_Cost_USD = Daily_Quality_Cost_USD * 250   // [USD/year] Annual quality cost (250 working days)
Cpk_Gap = target_cpk_val - Cpk   // [—] Gap to target (positive = improvement needed)
Centering_Index = 1 - ABS(2 * process_mean - usl - lsl) / Tolerance_Band   // [—] Process centering ratio (1.0 = perfectly centred)

standards: ASQ Six Sigma Body of Knowledge

usl_lsl_check.error_msg: LSL must be strictly less than USL. Check specification limits — bilateral tolerance requires LSL < target < USL.

std_dev_positive.error_msg: Standard deviation cannot be zero or negative. Zero variation is physically impossible in any real process.

mean_outside_spec.error_msg: Process mean is outside specification limits. PPM = 500,000+. Process is fundamentally out of control — all output may be non-conforming. Stop production and investigate immediately.

sample_minimum.error_msg: AIAG SPC requires minimum 25 subgroups for statistically valid Cpk. Current sample insufficient — Cpk estimate unreliable.

smart_warnings.message: PROCESS INCAPABLE — Cpk < 1.0: Process cannot meet specification. Defect rate is measured in percent, not PPM. Immediate containment (100% inspection) mandatory. Root cause analysis and process redesign required before resuming normal production.

CAPABILITY BELOW REQUIREMENT: Cpk is below the required target. Customer PPAP submission will be rejected. For automotive (IATF 16949): Cpk ≥ 1.33 is mandatory for production approval. Variance reduction required.

PROCESS OFF-CENTRE: Cp is significantly higher than Cpk. Process has good potential width but is shifted from nominal. Re-centering alone (tool offset, fixture adjustment) could reduce PPM dramatically without improving process variation.

smart_warnings.source: AIAG SPC — Cp vs Pp Analysis
smart_warnings.message: SIGNIFICANT Cpk–Ppk GAP (>0.3): Long-term variation substantially exceeds short-term. Special causes (tool wear, material lot changes, operator shifts, temperature drift) are present. SPC control chart review and MSA re-evaluation required.

Annual quality cost exceeds $50,000: This Cpk level is generating significant financial loss. Investment in process improvement, SPC implementation, or machine capability upgrade will deliver measurable ROI. Present to management with this annual cost figure.

Poor process centering (index < 0.70): Mean is more than 30% of tolerance from nominal. Asymmetric defect distribution — one side generating significantly more rejects. Investigate systematic bias: fixture wear, tool offset error, or raw material dimensional shift.

fmea.effect: LSL must be strictly less than USL. Check specification limits — bilateral tolerance requires LSL < target < USL.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: process_mean > usl || process_mean < lsl
fmea.effect: Process mean is outside specification limits. PPM = 500,000+. Process is fundamentally out of control — all output may be non-conforming. Stop production and investigate immediately.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: AIAG SPC requires minimum 25 subgroups for statistically valid Cpk. Current sample insufficient — Cpk estimate unreliable.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: PROCESS INCAPABLE — Cpk < 1.0: Process cannot meet specification. Defect rate is measured in percent, not PPM. Immediate containment (100% inspection) mandatory. Root cause analysis and process redesign required before resuming normal production.

CAPABILITY BELOW REQUIREMENT: Cpk is below the required target. Customer PPAP submission will be rejected. For automotive (IATF 16949): Cpk ≥ 1.33 is mandatory for production approval. Variance reduction required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Cpk >= 1.0 && Cpk < target_cpk_val

fmea.failureMode: PROCESS OFF-CENTRE: Cp is significantly higher than Cpk. Process has good potential width but is shifted from nominal. Re-centering alone (tool offset, fixture adjustment) could reduce PPM dramatically without improving process variation.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Cp > Cpk * 1.33

fmea.failureMode: SIGNIFICANT Cpk–Ppk GAP (>0.3): Long-term variation substantially exceeds short-term. Special causes (tool wear, material lot changes, operator shifts, temperature drift) are present. SPC control chart review and MSA re-evaluation required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Cpk > 0 && Ppk > 0 && (Cpk - Ppk) > 0.3
fmea.control_measure: AIAG SPC — Cp vs Pp Analysis

fmea.failureMode: Annual quality cost exceeds $50,000: This Cpk level is generating significant financial loss. Investment in process improvement, SPC implementation, or machine capability upgrade will deliver measurable ROI. Present to management with this annual cost figure.

Poor process centering (index < 0.70): Mean is more than 30% of tolerance from nominal. Asymmetric defect distribution — one side generating significantly more rejects. Investigate systematic bias: fixture wear, tool offset error, or raw material dimensional shift.
